title: Functional Assessments Guide
description: Evidence-based functional assessment tools for screening mobility, balance, strength and frailty risk in older adults.

blocks.headline: Functional Assessments Guide
blocks.subtitle: Standardized tests of gait, balance and strength predict falls, frailty and functional decline. Learn which assessments to use and how to interpret results.
blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Patient running an Able Assess screening

blocks.body:
Functional assessments are objective performance tests that measure physical capacity across domains such as mobility, balance, strength and endurance. These timed or observation-based tests provide clinically actionable information about fall risk, frailty status and likelihood of functional decline in older adults. Able Care integrates validated assessment protocols into digital workflows, enabling care organizations to screen multiple individuals systematically, record standardized measurements and flag high-risk individuals for intervention. Tests like the Timed Up and Go, 30-Second Chair Stand and 4-Meter Walk Test require minimal equipment and take minutes to administer, yet demonstrate strong predictive validity for adverse events. Whether you're implementing falls prevention programs in senior living, identifying candidates for frailty interventions in home care or conducting population health screenings, functional assessments provide objective, evidence-based data that drives clinical decision-making.

Articles in this guide

  • Functional Assessments for Older Adults - overview of validated testing battery
  • The Timed Up and Go (TUG) Test - mobility and fall risk assessment
  • Sit-to-Stand Test - lower extremity strength and power measurement
  • Gait Speed and the 4-Meter Walk Test - walking velocity and mortality prediction
  • 30-Second Chair Stand Test - rapid assessment of leg strength
  • Objective Falls Risk Assessment in Under 5 Minutes - efficient multi-domain screening
  • Sarcopenia Screening - identifying age-related muscle loss
  • Frailty Assessment Tools Compared - Fried phenotype, FRAIL scale and alternatives

blocks.heading: Integrate functional assessment screening into your workflows
blocks.bodyText: Able Care's assessment platform guides staff through standardized tests and automatically interprets results against age-matched norms.
